name: Level Up
description: A threshold-crossing event that increases a player, character, account, or system level and grants associated rewards.
design_notes: Level up is the moment where accumulated progress becomes a state change. It should define level_threshold_table crossing, reward grant order, multiple-level behavior, stat recalculation, unlock notifications, and migration rules for curve changes.
design_purpose: Create clear milestones in a longer progression curve.
Bundle rewards such as stats, skill points, abilities, or access gates into understandable moments.
Give players a reason to continue after each session by previewing the next level.
player_fantasy: The player feels a clear jump in capability, status, or possibility when effort reaches a milestone.

parameters.name: level_threshold_table
parameters.description: Ordered cumulative progress values required for each level.

parameters.name: level_reward_table_id
parameters.description: Reward table used when a specific level is reached.

parameters.name: max_level_cap
parameters.description: Highest level currently supported by the progression track.

parameters.name: skill_points_per_level
parameters.description: Number of spendable points granted for each eligible level-up.

parameters.name: retroactive_reward_policy
parameters.description: Handling for rewards added to levels the player already passed.

edge_cases: A single XP grant crosses several levels and must apply every stat and reward in order.
A max level cap increases after players stored overflow XP or reached the old cap.
Level rewards change in a patch and existing saves need retroactive_reward_policy handling.
A level-up occurs while in combat, dialogue, or matchmaking and notification timing must not interrupt control.
common_bugs: Only one level reward is granted during multi-level gains.
Derived stats recalculate before equipment or skill modifiers are reloaded, causing temporary wrong values.
Level-up notifications play before persistence, so a crash loses the reward but not the memory of seeing it.
Client predicts a level-up and unlocks UI that the server later rejects.
balancing_notes: Early levels should arrive quickly enough to teach the loop; later thresholds can stretch only if rewards stay desirable.
Large power jumps need content difficulty, gear, and enemy scaling to account for the new level state.
Previewing next-level rewards helps players judge whether the grind is worth continuing.
accessibility_notes: Provide a reviewable summary of level rewards after the animation finishes.
Do not rely only on flashing effects or loud audio to communicate a level-up.

implementation_notes.unity: Process level-up inside the progression service after XP commit: loop thresholds, grant level_reward_table_id rewards, recalc stats, persist level_state, then notify MonoBehaviour/UI listeners.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep level_threshold_table and reward tables as data Resources; update level_state through a deterministic service and emit signals only after save-ready state changes.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use PlayerState or a progression subsystem for authoritative level changes; Gameplay Ability or stat grants should be applied from committed reward payloads, not from UI events.
implementation_notes.web: Represent threshold crossing as a pure function returning new level, rewards, and overflow XP; persist the result before rendering celebration UI.

relationships.reason: Level-up thresholds consume or evaluate accumulated experience points to advance character state.
Level gains can grant skill points or prerequisite access for skill tree nodes.
Level milestones often grant new abilities, slots, or upgrade eligibility.

example_games.title: Dragon Quest
example_games.note: Uses level-ups to grant stats and progression milestones.

example_games.title: World of Warcraft
example_games.note: Uses character levels to gate content and abilities.
